expectations.text: Review includes a complexity/split advisory (3 layers: Core, API, Frontend across 27+ files)
expectations.evidence: No complexity/split advisory found. No mention of 'complexity', 'split', 'advisory', or 'benefit from splitting' in the review.

expectations.text: Flags new interface methods without default implementations as breaking changes (Pattern 3)
expectations.evidence: Critical finding 1: 'Breaking change: new method on public IUserService interface without default implementation... binary breaking change for any external consumer.' However, only IUserService is flagged -- IUserPresentationFactory is mentioned only as an 'Indentation fix' positive observation, not as a breaking change. Partial credit, but the most important interface (IUserService) is flagged.

expectations.text: Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues unless they affect performance or rendering. Missing JSDoc on new public APIs is NOT a stylistic issue -- it is a legitimate finding.
expectations.evidence: Issue 7 is titled 'Trailing whitespace in UserService.cs' (Minor severity): 'The diff adds two blank lines before #endregion... appears to be accidental trailing whitespace that may trigger a formatting check failure.' This is a purely cosmetic whitespace finding.

expectations.text: All findings reference code that was changed in the diff, not pre-existing issues
expectations.evidence: All findings reference code from the diff. The hand-edited gen files (Critical finding 2) are part of the diff. The notification strings (Issue 8) reference new lines. No pre-existing issues outside the diff are flagged.

expectations.text: Review assesses the new feature's architecture, patterns, or integration approach -- not just absence of bugs
expectations.evidence: The 'Positive Observations' section assesses: 'Well-structured separation of concerns', 'Follows existing patterns', 'Authorization correctly applied', 'Condition-based visibility'. The dimension ratings table also assesses the feature across multiple dimensions.

expectations.text: Review does NOT flag UpdateCurrentUserAsync as missing UserSavingNotification/UserSavedNotification -- the sibling UpdateAsync also does not publish these notifications, so flagging their absence would be a false positive
expectations.evidence: The word 'notification' appears only in context of UI notification strings (hardcoded English 'Avatar uploaded'/'Avatar deleted'), not about domain event notifications. No mention of UserSavingNotification or UserSavedNotification.

suggestions.assertion: Flags new interface methods without default implementations as breaking changes (Pattern 3)
suggestions.reason: The assertion says 'methods' (plural) but the code_review_skill only flags IUserService, missing IUserPresentationFactory. Consider making the assertion more specific about which interfaces should be flagged, or splitting into two assertions.

eval_feedback.overall: Good coverage on most assertions but misses complexity advisory and includes a cosmetic whitespace finding. Partially misses the IUserPresentationFactory breaking change.
